task: I’ve got a coworker trying to dump the closing jobs on me again right as the shift is winding down. I need a reply that keeps it from becoming a back-and-forth while still making the handoff clear.

turns.agent_response:
Send:

**“I can’t take on closing tonight. I’m tied up with my section.”**

If you want it a little firmer:
**“I can’t pick up closing tonight — I’m staying on my section.”**

If you want, I can make it sound more **blunt**, **polite**, or **text-like**.

user_simulator.text:
Too soft. That sounds like you’re asking permission.

Need it cleaner, like:

“I’m not taking closing. I’m on my section till I’m done.”

Or if you want it shut down harder:

“Can’t do closing. My section comes first.”

Short. No explaining. The more you talk, the more they push.

turns.agent_response:
Use:

**“Not taking closing. I’m on my section till I’m done.”**

If you want it even harder:
**“Can’t take closing. I’m on my section.”**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s better. Clean enough.

“Not taking closing. I’m on my section till I’m done.”

Short, no opening for a debate. If they keep pressing, just repeat it. Don’t start explaining yourself — that’s where people smell weakness.
